questions.question: What does the `super` keyword refer to in Java inheritance?
options: A) The parent class
B) The current class
C) The superclass
D) The child class
questions.explanation: The `super` keyword refers to the parent class in Java inheritance.

questions.question: A class cannot have multiple parents, what is this concept called?
options: A) Multilevel inheritance
B) Multiparent inheritance
C) Single inheritance
D) Hierarchical inheritance
questions.explanation: This concept is called Single inheritance.

questions.question: What happens when a child class has a constructor with the same name as the parent class?
options: A) The child class constructor is ignored
B) The parent class constructor is overridden
C) The child class constructor is called before the parent class constructor
D) The child class constructor is called after the parent class constructor
questions.explanation: When a child class has a constructor with the same name as the parent class, the parent class constructor is overridden.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `super()` method in a child class constructor?
options: A) To call the parent class constructor
B) To initialize the child class variables
C) To overload the parent class constructor
D) To create a new object
questions.explanation: The purpose of the `super()` method in a child class constructor is to call the parent class constructor.

questions.question: Can a child class have a constructor with no parameters, and then call the parent class constructor with parameters?
options: A) Yes, it can
B) No, it cannot
C) It depends on the parent class
D) It depends on the child class
questions.explanation: Yes, a child class can have a constructor with no parameters, and then call the parent class constructor with parameters.
